title: Update working memory from macOS shortcut display session
summary: Durable context learned while adapting popup shortcut labels and input-collapse docs for macOS modifier display.

claims.text: Browser detection utilities expose `isMac()` for macOS detection via `navigator.platform` with a user-agent fallback, and `getModifierKey()` returns `âŒ˜` on macOS and `Ctrl` elsewhere for shortcut labels.

`KeyboardShortcutService.formatShortcut()` now renders modifier keys with platform-specific labels: macOS uses `âŒ˜`, `âŒ¥`, `âŒƒ`, and `â‡§` with no separator, while other platforms keep text labels and map `Meta` to `Win` with ` + ` separators.

The runtime shortcut handlers accept either Ctrl or Meta for the affected shortcuts: send behavior sends on Ctrl/Cmd+Enter, and input collapse expands a collapsed input on Ctrl/Cmd+I. The macOS display session changed labels and discoverability rather than the shortcut mechanics.

The popup settings UI replaces `{modifier}` with `getModifierKey()` for `ctrlEnterSend` and `ctrlEnterSendHint`, and it appends the localized `inputCollapseShortcutHint` beside the input-collapse enable description.

Shortcut-related extension messages use a `{modifier}` placeholder instead of hardcoded `Ctrl` for `ctrlEnterSend` and `ctrlEnterSendHint`, and include `inputCollapseShortcutHint` for the collapsed-input expansion shortcut across the locale message files.
The localized VitePress input-collapse guide pages document the expansion shortcut as `Ctrl`/`âŒ˜`+`I` in a static markdown form.
Ctrl/Cmd+I should remain visible in the popup input-collapse settings because the popup is the feature entry point and this keeps shortcut discoverability consistent with the Ctrl/Cmd+Enter setting.
VitePress input-collapse docs should use the platform-agnostic static notation `Ctrl`/`âŒ˜`+`I` for the expansion shortcut instead of runtime platform detection.

sources.title: Popup and localized release UI changes

metadata.reason: The user questioned whether Ctrl+I should be indicated in the popup; the session kept it there for discoverability and consistency, and the user accepted that direction before requesting docs coverage.

After accepting the popup hint, the user asked to add the shortcut to VitePress docs if missing, and the session chose static `Ctrl`/`âŒ˜` notation because markdown docs do not run platform detection.
